faqs.question: What types of power solutions does Xinleineng offer for industrial applications?
faqs.answer: Xinleineng provides comprehensive power solutions including industrial motor drive systems, solar inverter power solutions, EV charging infrastructure, and UPS systems. These solutions leverage their IGBT modules, SiC MOSFET modules, and intelligent power modules to deliver high efficiency and reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for personalized solution recommendations based on your specific requirements.

faqs.question: How can I get technical support for implementing Xinleineng power modules in my design?
faqs.answer: BeiLuo Electronics provides comprehensive technical support for Xinleineng power module implementation. Our FAE team offers schematic review, thermal design consultation, gate drive circuit optimization, and troubleshooting assistance. Contact our technical support team or submit a support ticket through our website.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for personalized solution recommendations based on your specific requirements.

faqs.question: What are the key advantages of using Xinleineng SiC modules in high-frequency applications?
faqs.answer: Xinleineng SiC modules offer significant advantages for high-frequency applications including: 1) Lower switching losses enabling higher switching frequencies (up to 100kHz+), 2) Reduced cooling requirements due to higher efficiency, 3) Smaller magnetic components and filters, 4) Higher power density in the final system, and 5) Improved overall system efficiency by 2-5% compared to IGBT solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for personalized solution recommendations based on your specific requirements.

faqs.question: Does Xinleineng provide reference designs for their power modules?
faqs.answer: Yes, Xinleineng provides comprehensive reference designs including complete schematics, PCB layout guidelines, BOM lists, and thermal management recommendations. These reference designs are available for motor drives, solar inverters, EV chargers, and welding equipment applications. Contact BeiLuo Electronics to request specific reference designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for personalized solution recommendations based on your specific requirements.

faqs.question: What thermal management solutions are recommended for Xinleineng power modules?
faqs.answer: Xinleineng power modules require proper thermal management including: 1) Heatsinks with thermal resistance appropriate for the application, 2) Thermal interface materials with low thermal resistance, 3) Forced air cooling or liquid cooling for high-power applications, 4) Proper mounting torque (typically 2-4 Nm), and 5) Thermal simulation to verify junction temperatures remain within limits under all operating conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for personalized solution recommendations based on your specific requirements.

faqs.question: How do I select the right gate driver for Xinleineng IGBT and SiC modules?
faqs.answer: Gate driver selection depends on the module type: For IGBT modules, use drivers with +15V/-8V output and minimum 4A peak current. For SiC modules, use +18V/-4V or +15V/-4V drivers with 6A+ peak current capability. Key considerations include isolation voltage (minimum 2500V), propagation delay (under 100ns), and protection features like desaturation detection and soft shutdown.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for personalized solution recommendations based on your specific requirements.

faqs.question: What is the typical lead time for Xinleineng power modules?
faqs.answer: Standard lead time for Xinleineng power modules is 8-12 weeks for production quantities. For high-volume orders, long-term supply agreements can be established to secure capacity and reduce lead times. BeiLuo Electronics maintains inventory of popular models for immediate delivery. Contact our sales team for current availability and lead time information.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for personalized solution recommendations based on your specific requirements.

faqs.question: Are Xinleineng power modules qualified for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: Xinleineng is developing AEC-Q101 qualified power modules for automotive applications. Currently, their industrial-grade modules are widely used in EV charging infrastructure and industrial EV applications. For specific automotive qualification requirements, contact BeiLuo Electronics to discuss your application needs and qualification status.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for personalized solution recommendations based on your specific requirements.

solutions.title: Industrial Motor Drive Solution
solutions.description: High-performance motor drive solution using Xinleineng IGBT and IPM modules for industrial automation applications.
solutions.longDescription:
The Industrial Control Solution 1 from xinleineng represents a comprehensive approach to addressing modern challenges in Industrial. This solution integrates advanced semiconductor technology with robust design methodologies to deliver exceptional performance and reliability.

At the core of this solution is a carefully selected portfolio of xinleineng components optimized for the target application. The design leverages the latest advances in power management, signal processing, and thermal management to achieve industry-leading efficiency and performance metrics.

Key technical features include high-efficiency power conversion, precise control algorithms, and comprehensive protection mechanisms. The solution supports wide operating ranges and maintains performance across varying environmental conditions. Advanced monitoring and diagnostic capabilities enable predictive maintenance and optimize system uptime.

Implementation is streamlined through comprehensive design support including reference designs, simulation models, and detailed application notes. BeiLuo's FAE team provides expert guidance throughout the development cycle from concept through production. Customization options are available to address specific application requirements.

The solution has been validated through extensive testing including functional verification, environmental stress screening, and long-term reliability assessment. Field deployment data demonstrates consistent performance and high customer satisfaction across diverse applications and operating conditions.

faqs.question: What motor power range does this solution support?
faqs.answer: The Industrial Motor Drive Solution supports motor power from 1kW to 75kW using different Xinleineng module configurations. For 1-7.5kW applications, the XLN-IPM15A or XLN-IPM30A intelligent power modules provide integrated gate drive and protection. For 11-75kW applications, discrete IGBT modules (XLN75T120, XLN150T120) with external gate drivers offer higher power handling and design flexibility.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for detailed application guidance.

faqs.question: What control algorithms are supported by this motor drive solution?
faqs.answer: The solution supports various motor control algorithms including V/Hz control for simple applications, sensorless vector control (FOC) for high-performance applications, and encoder-based closed-loop vector control for precision positioning. The fast switching characteristics of Xinleineng IGBT modules enable accurate current control with low harmonic distortion.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for detailed application guidance.

faqs.question: How does the solution handle regenerative braking?
faqs.answer: The motor drive solution supports regenerative braking through active front-end (AFE) rectifier configuration or braking chopper circuits. For applications with frequent braking, the AFE option using Xinleineng IGBT modules enables energy return to the grid with unity power factor. For cost-sensitive applications, a braking chopper with external resistor provides simple braking capability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for detailed application guidance.

faqs.question: What protection features are included in the IPM-based motor drives?
faqs.answer: Xinleineng IPM modules integrate comprehensive protection features including: Undervoltage lockout (UVLO) for gate drive, Overcurrent protection (OCP) with desaturation detection, Overtemperature protection (OTP) with internal NTC thermistor, and fault output signaling. These built-in protections simplify drive design and improve system reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for detailed application guidance.

faqs.question: What is the efficiency of this motor drive solution at typical operating points?
faqs.answer: The motor drive solution achieves 96-98% efficiency at rated load when using Xinleineng IGBT modules. At light loads (25-50%), efficiency remains above 94% due to the low conduction and switching losses of the power modules. Using SiC modules in the rectifier stage can further improve overall system efficiency by 1-2%.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for detailed application guidance.

faqs.question: What EMC considerations are important for this motor drive design?
faqs.answer: Key EMC considerations include: 1) Proper gate resistor selection to control dv/dt and reduce EMI, 2) Shielded motor cables or output filters to reduce conducted emissions, 3) DC link capacitors with low ESL for high-frequency noise suppression, 4) Proper grounding and shielding practices, and 5) Input filters to meet IEC 61800-3 requirements for adjustable speed electrical power drive systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for detailed application guidance.

faeInsights.logic: Motor drive applications require careful consideration of thermal management, switching losses, and protection features. Xinleineng's IPM modules simplify design for low-to-medium power applications, while discrete IGBT modules offer flexibility for higher power designs. The key is matching the module characteristics to the application's switching frequency and thermal requirements.
keyPoints: IPM modules integrate gate drive and protection, reducing design complexity for <7.5kW applications
Discrete IGBT modules offer higher current capability and design flexibility for >11kW drives
Thermal design must account for worst-case conditions including blocked airflow and maximum ambient temperature
Gate resistor selection balances switching losses against EMI and voltage overshoot
Current sensing methods (shunt resistor vs. isolated sensor) affect protection response time
pitfalls: Insufficient heatsink thermal capacity leads to overtemperature shutdown under peak load conditions
Inadequate gate drive current causes slow switching and increased switching losses
Long gate lead lengths introduce parasitic inductance causing voltage oscillations
Missing RC snubbers across module terminals can result in excessive voltage spikes
Improper dead-time setting in PWM control causes shoot-through failures
bestPractices: Perform thermal simulation with actual operating profiles including overload conditions
Use isolated gate drivers with built-in protection features for high-power designs
Implement soft-start for DC bus capacitors to limit inrush current
Include dv/dt filters for motor cable lengths exceeding 50 meters
Design PCB with low-inductance DC bus connections using laminated busbars for high current
faeInsights.guidelines: Follow these guidelines for successful implementation: 1) Use recommended gate drivers, 2) Implement proper thermal design, 3) Follow layout guidelines, 4) Test under all operating conditions
faeInsights.content: Based on extensive experience supporting customers with Industrial Motor Drive Solution, this solution addresses critical design challenges through proven architecture. The implementation achieves optimal balance between performance, reliability, and cost-effectiveness.

customerCases.title: CNC Machine Tool Spindle Drive
customerCases.challenge: A CNC machine manufacturer needed a 15kW spindle drive with wide constant power range (1:4), fast dynamic response for acceleration/deceleration, and high reliability for 24/7 operation in harsh industrial environments with high dust and vibration levels.
customerCases.solution: Implemented a three-phase inverter using XLN150T120 IGBT modules with external high-current gate drivers. The solution included regenerative braking capability, advanced thermal management with liquid cooling, and comprehensive protection features.
results: Achieved 150% overload capacity for 1 minute during heavy cutting operations
Reduced spindle acceleration time by 30% compared to previous solution
System efficiency improved to 97.2% at rated load
MTBF increased to over 50,000 hours with proper thermal design
Passed EMC Class A compliance on first submission
customerCases.testimonial: The Xinleineng IGBT modules provided excellent performance in our demanding CNC spindle application. The low switching losses and robust thermal performance allowed us to achieve higher power density while maintaining reliability.
customerCases.contact: Chief Electrical Engineer, Precision Machine Tools Ltd.
customerCases.feedback: The Xinleineng IGBT modules provided excellent performance in our demanding CNC spindle application. The low switching losses and robust thermal performance allowed us to achieve higher power density while maintaining reliability.
customerCases.result: Achieved significant performance improvement with enhanced reliability.

customerCases.title: HVAC Fan and Pump Drive Retrofit
customerCases.challenge: A commercial building needed to retrofit 50 fan and pump drives with energy-efficient variable frequency drives. The project required compact size for existing electrical rooms, easy installation by maintenance staff, and compatibility with existing building management systems.
customerCases.solution: Deployed drives using XLN-IPM30A intelligent power modules with integrated gate drive and protection. The compact IPM-based design reduced drive size by 40%, while the built-in protections simplified installation and commissioning.
results: 40% reduction in drive enclosure size compared to discrete designs
Energy savings of 35-50% depending on load profile
Installation time reduced by 60% due to simplified wiring
Zero field failures in first 18 months of operation
ROI achieved in 14 months through energy savings
customerCases.testimonial: The IPM-based solution was perfect for our retrofit project. The compact size fit our existing electrical rooms, and the integrated protections made installation straightforward for our maintenance team.
customerCases.feedback: The IPM-based solution was perfect for our retrofit project. The compact size fit our existing electrical rooms, and the integrated protections made installation straightforward for our maintenance team.
customerCases.result: Achieved significant performance improvement with enhanced reliability.

solutions.name: Industrial Control Solution 1

solutions.title: Solar Inverter Power Solution
solutions.description: High-efficiency solar inverter solution using Xinleineng SiC and IGBT modules for residential and commercial PV systems.
solutions.longDescription:
The Automotive Electronics Solution 2 from xinleineng represents a comprehensive approach to addressing modern challenges in Industrial. This solution integrates advanced semiconductor technology with robust design methodologies to deliver exceptional performance and reliability.

At the core of this solution is a carefully selected portfolio of xinleineng components optimized for the target application. The design leverages the latest advances in power management, signal processing, and thermal management to achieve industry-leading efficiency and performance metrics.

Key technical features include high-efficiency power conversion, precise control algorithms, and comprehensive protection mechanisms. The solution supports wide operating ranges and maintains performance across varying environmental conditions. Advanced monitoring and diagnostic capabilities enable predictive maintenance and optimize system uptime.

Implementation is streamlined through comprehensive design support including reference designs, simulation models, and detailed application notes. BeiLuo's FAE team provides expert guidance throughout the development cycle from concept through production. Customization options are available to address specific application requirements.

The solution has been validated through extensive testing including functional verification, environmental stress screening, and long-term reliability assessment. Field deployment data demonstrates consistent performance and high customer satisfaction across diverse applications and operating conditions.

faqs.question: What efficiency levels can be achieved with this solar inverter solution?
faqs.answer: Using Xinleineng SiC modules in the DC-AC inverter stage, peak efficiency reaches 98.5% with CEC weighted efficiency above 98%. The low switching losses of SiC technology enable high switching frequencies (up to 50kHz) while maintaining high efficiency, reducing magnetic component size and cost.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for detailed application guidance.

faqs.question: What MPPT voltage range is supported by this solution?
faqs.answer: The solution supports wide MPPT voltage ranges: 200-850VDC for residential single-phase inverters and 200-1000VDC for commercial three-phase inverters. Using 1200V rated power modules ensures safe operation with adequate voltage margin even at maximum DC input voltage and temperature.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for detailed application guidance.

faqs.question: How does the solution handle reactive power and grid support functions?
faqs.answer: The inverter solution supports reactive power injection (leading and lagging) from 0.8 leading to 0.8 lagging power factor. Grid support functions include active anti-islanding, voltage ride-through, frequency ride-through, and dynamic reactive power support per IEEE 1547 and IEC 61727 standards. Fast current control loops enable rapid response to grid voltage changes.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for detailed application guidance.

faqs.question: What are the thermal design considerations for outdoor installation?
faqs.answer: For outdoor installations, the solution uses IP65-rated enclosures with passive cooling (no fans) for residential units or forced air cooling with IP54-rated fans for commercial units. Power modules are derated above 45C ambient temperature. Heatsinks are anodized aluminum with thermal interface materials rated for 25-year outdoor exposure.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for detailed application guidance.

faqs.question: Can this solution be used for energy storage inverter applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, with modifications to the control software, the solution supports bidirectional power flow for battery energy storage systems (BESS). The same SiC and IGBT power stages can operate in both inverter (DC to AC) and rectifier (AC to DC) modes. Battery voltage ranges of 400-800VDC are supported with appropriate DC-DC converter stages.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for detailed application guidance.

faqs.question: What is the expected service life of the power modules in solar inverter applications?
faqs.answer: Xinleineng power modules are designed for 25-year service life in solar applications. With proper thermal design keeping junction temperatures below 100C under normal operating conditions, the modules exceed IEC 61215 thermal cycling requirements. Accelerated life testing predicts less than 0.1% annual failure rate over 25 years.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for detailed application guidance.

faeInsights.logic: Solar inverter applications demand the highest possible efficiency to maximize energy harvest. SiC modules are the optimal choice for the DC-AC stage due to their low switching losses at high frequencies. The wide bandgap properties of SiC also provide better high-temperature performance, which is critical for outdoor installations. Careful attention to thermal design and EMI filtering is essential for reliable 25-year operation.
keyPoints: SiC modules enable 98.5%+ peak efficiency with reduced cooling requirements
High switching frequency (30-50kHz) reduces filter inductor size and cost
Wide temperature operation (-40C to +60C ambient) requires careful thermal design
Grid-tie compliance requires careful filter design for THD <3%
Arc fault detection and rapid shutdown are required by NEC 2017/2020
pitfalls: Insufficient DC link capacitance causes voltage ripple that degrades MPPT performance
Inadequate common-mode filtering results in ground leakage current exceeding safety limits
Poor thermal interface material selection leads to thermal runaway after years of outdoor exposure
Missing surge protection on DC input exposes modules to lightning damage
Inadequate gate drive decoupling causes erratic switching at high temperatures
bestPractices: Use film capacitors for DC link (not electrolytic) for 25-year life
Implement interleaved switching for multi-phase inverters to reduce ripple
Design heatsinks with natural convection for residential, forced air for commercial
Include DC arc fault detection circuit per UL 1699B requirements
Use conformal coating on all PCBs for humidity protection in outdoor environments
faeInsights.guidelines: Follow these guidelines for successful implementation: 1) Use recommended gate drivers, 2) Implement proper thermal design, 3) Follow layout guidelines, 4) Test under all operating conditions
faeInsights.content: Based on extensive experience supporting customers with Solar Inverter Power Solution, this solution addresses critical design challenges through proven architecture. The implementation achieves optimal balance between performance, reliability, and cost-effectiveness.

customerCases.title: Commercial Rooftop Solar Installation
customerCases.challenge: A solar EPC company needed a 50kW three-phase inverter solution for commercial rooftop installations. Requirements included 98.5%+ efficiency, wide MPPT voltage range (250-850V), compact size for rooftop mounting, and compliance with local grid codes including reactive power support.
customerCases.solution: Designed a three-phase inverter using XLN40M120-SiC modules for the DC-AC stage and XLN-RECT35A modules for the active front-end boost converter. The SiC-based design achieved target efficiency while reducing heatsink size by 50% compared to IGBT alternatives.
results: Peak efficiency of 98.7% measured at full load
50% reduction in heatsink volume compared to previous IGBT design
MPPT efficiency >99.5% across full voltage range
THD <1.5% at rated power, meeting strict grid codes
Passed all grid compliance tests including ride-through
customerCases.testimonial: The SiC module solution exceeded our efficiency targets while reducing the overall inverter size. Our customers appreciate the higher energy harvest and easier rooftop installation.
customerCases.feedback: The SiC module solution exceeded our efficiency targets while reducing the overall inverter size. Our customers appreciate the higher energy harvest and easier rooftop installation.
customerCases.result: Achieved significant performance improvement with enhanced reliability.

customerCases.title: Residential Energy Storage System
customerCases.challenge: An energy storage company needed a 10kW hybrid inverter for residential battery systems. The inverter needed to support both solar PV input and battery bidirectional operation, with silent operation (no fans) and high efficiency across wide load range.
customerCases.solution: Developed a hybrid inverter using XLN80M120-SiC modules for the bidirectional DC-AC stage. The high efficiency of SiC enabled passive cooling up to 8kW, with a low-speed fan only activating above 8kW or high ambient temperatures.
results: Round-trip efficiency (battery to AC) of 96.5%
Silent operation below 8kW output (no fans)
European efficiency of 97.8% across typical load profile
Compact wall-mount design (60% smaller than competitors)
Zero fan noise complaints from homeowners
customerCases.testimonial: The SiC-based design allowed us to create a truly silent hybrid inverter that homeowners love. The efficiency gains translate directly to lower electricity bills for our customers.
customerCases.feedback: The SiC-based design allowed us to create a truly silent hybrid inverter that homeowners love. The efficiency gains translate directly to lower electricity bills for our customers.
customerCases.result: Achieved significant performance improvement with enhanced reliability.
